solutions.solution: The word 'professional' has not come anywhere in the statement. It says the way the author says that he doesn't want to become an employee. But he says it may' implies that one wants to become something which he is not currently. So I is not implicit.

The phrase 'an average layman can study' is not used to mean that laymen cannot study but just to say that it means that he or she can study. The statement is only meant to highlight the good qualities of the wants. The statement can bring his problem but at the same time, in the same line he may not be having any problem: I may be false. Similarly, the tone of the statement implies as if the author wants the author to solve the problem. It does not necessarily imply that the author can solve any problem.

Neither can we say for sure that the scheme is successful unless we know how much procurement is targeted. If I .d lakh was the target, it's a gross failure, so we are not sure. Similarly, we cannot say with certainty that no procurement should be done now because we do not know whether the procurement has been successful or short of target.

Both the statements, I truly, be author expresses surprise that despite more commuters being there, more buses are not being demanded; this implies that the author believes that the number of buses in a line depends upon the number of commuters. Secondly, the author's tone of surprise implies that people do not usually tolerate inconvenience.

The statement says that if people ask about your tailor, it indicates a good suit. This anonymously means that if you are not wearing a good suit, they will not ask about your tailor. It is not implicit. The statement talks about a criterion of a good suit. It does not talk about people's desire to know about the criterion.

Since inequality can be reduced by man's actions, it must be a man-made phenomenon. But the eradication of inequality does not necessarily mean merely making the poor rich with what he possesses. We may reduce it. I is not implicit because that would mean that fascism or communism have just one meaning and that is denying the rights of the individual. Similarly, II is not implicit because the word mean that fascism and communism succeed only when people have suffered from poverty and deprivation. This may not be true.

If I was assumed by the incharge, he would not recommend conveyance allowance for employees. He wants to give reward (of conveyance allowance) in order to bring in discipline (punctuality). This means that it is implicit.

Since penalty has been provided for, it means that there must be some misuse of alarm chain, so I is implicit. Since the alarm chain has not been taken off despite its misuse, it means that people may genuinely want to stop a train on some occasions. Hence, II is implicit.

It is definitely implicit because otherwise there wouldn't have been the advertisement. But it is not necessarily implicit. The reader may or may not be a classical singer. Otherwise, they would not have used the word 'if' in the statement, they would simply have said: 'We have a challenging job for you.'

The successful man has the ability to judge himself correctly. But this does not mean that the ability to judge himself correctly is the only cause of his success. This means that the ability to judge others may not be useless for him. Hence, I is not implicit. Similarly, II is also doubtful for similar reasons.

I is implicit because had this not been so, the supervisor couldn't have thought of asking the officer not to grant leave. It is implicit because had this not been so, the supervisor wouldn't have been in a position to say that there was some 'exigency of work'.

Since the card says that the invitee should take his seat before 3:00 p.m., it implies that the function will start as scheduled. It is the invitee who will have to see that he reaches before 3:00 p.m.

Remember the rule: In an advertisement, what is being highlighted must be desired by people. This makes I implicit. The statement does not say that other pipes are not durable. It only says that they are less durable. So, II is not implicit.

Since the job is not 'confirmed' without the review of the performance at the end of the probation period, it implies that at the beginning the performance may not be known. So, I is implicit. If may be true. It is clear by the way the sentence relates 'confirmation' with 'performance'.

Even valuables locked in a cupboard can be stolen, but they would be more difficult to steal. It implies extra safety and not perfect safety, so I is not implicit. Whether stealing is a crime or not is not discussed in the statement.

The statement only says that X mosquito coils are good, but it does not say that it is 'better than any other'. So, I is not implicit. However, since the advertisement promises a 'good night's sleep', it must be a desirable thing. So, II is implicit.
What the statement says has no direct correlation with either of the statements. They may be true but they may be false as well. The statement does not make any mention of the word 'intelligence'.
